giant cell tumor.default_prompt: a radiograph of giant cell tumor
descriptions: Irregular sclerotic margins surrounding lytic area in tibia.
Cortical thinning and bulging noted at site of lytic lesion.
Prominent soft tissue mass extending beyond cortical boundary.
Lytic lesion with punctate calcifications in metaphyseal region.
Giant cell tumor exhibits central lucency within diaphysis.
Irregular erosive changes with scalloped margin along cortex.
Central expansile lesion with septations in distal femur.
Cortically-based expansile lesion in ulna with smooth margins.
Central sclerotic mass in distal radius with well-defined borders.
Sclerotic nodule at metaphyseal level of femur, no periosteal reaction.
Expansile lytic lesion in proximal tibia with intact cortex.
Cortical thickening with sclerotic center in humeral diaphysis.
Single sclerotic nodule in metacarpal head, no surrounding soft tissue.
Ovoid sclerotic mass in scaphoid with short pedunculated base.
Rapid bone expansion with soft tissue mass extending into knee joint.
Multiple discrete foci of lytic lesions in vertebral body.
Cortical destruction with sunburst periosteal reaction around diaphysis.
Osteolytic lesion with central sclerosis and surrounding reactive骨骺端溶骨性病变，中央伴有硬化灶和周围反应性新骨形成。

simple bone cyst.default_prompt: a radiograph of simple bone cyst
descriptions: Central lytic lesion in femoral shaft, clearly demarcated.
Diaphyseal lytic region in radius, no periosteal reaction.
Well-defined, solitary lytic spot in tibia without sclerosis.
Single, sharply defined lytic zone in ulna, central position.
Medullary cavity lytic lesion in fibula, smooth borders.
Simple lytic defect in distal femur, lacks sclerotic rim.
Unilocular lytic area in humeral diaphysis, uniform density.
Centrally located lytic spot in proximal phalanx of finger.
Calcified nodule with sclerotic rim in distal radius.
Soft tissue mass at knee with ground-glass matrix.
Lytic lesion in scapula with surrounding ground-glass density.
Well-defined lytic lesion with central calcification in femur shaft.
Cortical scalloping adjacent to expansile lytic lesion in tibia.
Irregularly shaped soft-tissue mass with internal calcifications near ankle.
Central calcification within a multilocular lytic lesion of humerus.
Eccentric lytic area with sclerotic margin in proximal fibula.
